Claim_Text: Label-free causal consensus improves held-out ORBIT user accuracy over the validation-selected fixed motif
Evidence_Source: EXP34 corrected official ORBIT CLU-VE; paired user-level bootstrap and exact sign-flip test
Notes: +0.0293 [0.0155 0.0437]; Holm p=0.00189; strict untouched-first-look replication remains inconclusive after invalid v2 coverage

Claim_Text: The gain requires causal within-video belief state rather than a memoryless ensemble
Evidence_Source: EXP34 memoryless majority and eight-frame-delay interventions with Holm correction
Notes: All four registered controls passed; full motif bank is charged to every consensus condition

Claim_Text: A high-rank E/I carrier retains at least 90% of controller gain with closed low-dimensional dynamics and normal contraction
Evidence_Source: Future participating-carrier comparison after EXP34 formal support
Notes: Cannot be supported by matrix rank alone

Claim_Text: A sparse successor improves very-low-shot accuracy versus measured personalization compute
Evidence_Source: Future support-shot and sparse-execution sweep; MACs wall time and mutable-parameter audit
Notes: Current EXP34 full-bank gate provides no efficiency evidence and charges mean event L1 for all motifs

Claim_Text: ORBIT is a real-world personalized few-shot video benchmark with causal test constraints
Evidence_Source: Massiceti et al. ICCV 2021 plus official ORBIT protocol
Notes: Official source and code snapshot recorded in provenance

Claim_Text: Reusable dynamical motifs and gated fast-weight memories are plausible building blocks but do not establish actuator selection
Evidence_Source: Driscoll et al. 2024; Gated DeltaNet 2024/2026; Mamba-3 2026
Notes: Positioning only, not empirical support for C0
